title: Beef Rendang
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Tan Hsueh Yun-style beef rendang: rich, aromatic, with metric ingredients and exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
keywords: Tan Hsueh Yun beef rendang thermomix
intro: Tan Hsueh Yun's rendition of beef rendang is a masterclass in patience and spice. This Chef Aly Thermomix version captures the deep, complex flavours while making the method simple, metric and practical for TM6 and TM7 home cooks.
ingredients: 3 lemongrass stalks, white part only, cut into 3 cm pieces
3 dried chillies, soaked in warm water 10 min, drained
600 g beef chuck, cut into 3 cm pieces
steps: Place shallots, garlic, ginger, lemongrass, soaked chillies, coriander, cumin and turmeric in the mixing bowl. Chop 10 sec / speed 7. Scrape down and repeat if needed to form a rough paste.
Add vegetable oil and cook 3 min / 120°C / speed 2 to bloom the spices.
Add beef pieces and stir 2 min / Reverse / speed 1 to coat in the paste.
Add coconut cream, water, kaffir lime leaf, salt and sugar. Cook 50 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed soft, with the simmering basket on the lid instead of the measuring cup.
Remove the simmering basket and continue cooking 10 min / Varoma / Reverse / speed 1 to reduce and intensify the sauce, stirring occasionally with the spatula if needed.
Check the beef is tender and the sauce has thickened to a rich, dark coating. Rest 5 min before serving with steamed rice.
tips: Chuck or gravy beef works best — the long cook breaks down the connective tissue into silky richness.
The simmering basket on the lid prevents splatter while allowing evaporation, which is key to a proper rendang.
For an even darker, drier rendang, add an extra 10 min at Varoma temperature in the final step.

faq.q: Can I make Beef Rendang in a Thermomix TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The times, temperatures and speeds are specified for both TM6 and TM7. Use Reverse stirring throughout to keep the beef pieces intact.

faq.q: Can I use beef brisket instead of chuck?
faq.a: Yes. Brisket works well but may need 5–10 min longer. Check tenderness at the 50 min mark.
